calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-16-2015 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-09-2015 Continued To Sep-16-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-02-2015 Continued To Sep-09-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Of Preliminary Distribution And For Instructions
Off Calendar Dec-29-2014 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Order Of Preliminary Distribution And For Instructions
Off Calendar Dec-23-2014 Continued To Dec-29-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Roosevelt for and with Susan Hempstead.

Counsel was given the opportunity to have the matter continued so that he could provide authority as to how the decedent's will can restrict the use of a monetary gift by establishing terms of a scholarship fund and provide authority as to how this Court can change the terms of the scholarship. Counsel elected to present oral argument. The Petition for Preliminary Distribution and for Instructions was taken under submission.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Oct-03-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Roosevelt for and with Susan Hempstead.

The Court informed Counsel that it will not make an order on the Ashbury property as it has been sold by the Petitioner as Executor, that the issue of community property will have to be addressed in the Estate, that all of the proceeds will have to be inventoried and that a determination of distribution needs to be made. The Court also stated that it is prepared to grant that the 49ers and Giants seats licenses are personal property. The Petition for Spousal Property was taken under submission for review of the order.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Sep-09-2014 Continued To Sep-23-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Roosevelt for and with (decedents widow) Susan Hempstead.

Counsel provided further oral argument to support his position with regard to the tangible personal property being community property. Counsel also clarified that he is asking for the St. Francis property, the Ashbury property, the Vanguard and Deutsche accounts and the various stocks be deemed 1/2 community property and 1/2 to the estate. In addition, Mr. Roosevelt raised the issue of the one year anniversary coming up and interest beginning to run on the bequests. Mr. Roosevelt is to file a declaration indicating that there are sufficient assets to fully fund Andrews trust and bequest. The petition for spousal property was taken under submission. The court expressed concern about the Ashbury property being sold as part of the estate and stated that it should remain as part of the estate.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Jun-11-2014 Continued To Aug-06-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Roosevelt for and with Susan Hempstead.

The Court stated that the matter is on for hearing because it does not feel it has a good grasp of the Decedent's inheritance from his Parents, that the some accounts are co-mingled, that some accounts are in Ms. Hempstead's name and raised questions about the Ashbury property/title of the property and other inheritances the Decedent received. Mr. Roosevelt stated that the Ashbury property was sold and monetized, that it was held in the original form of title in the Decedent's name as a single man, that it was an asset that existed on date of death and that another property was purchased. Mr. Roosevelt also stated that there is no way of determining the source of the 49ers license seat and the Giants license seat purchases, that it would be extremely difficult to separate 30 plus years of assets and costly to hire a forensic accountant, and that all four Beneficiaries are in the spirit of the relief requested. The Court pointed out that the petition does not disclose that the property was sold, stated that the property was the Decedent's separate property, that at the very least it would have to be a Moore Marsden calculation, questioned whether the Son of the Decedent has full understanding, and stated that the Court needs to follow the law and needs evidence that supports its ruling. The Petition for Spousal Property was continued to August 6, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for further support.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
